name: the-beak-of-the-finch
description: Jonathan Weiner's The Beak of the Finch — a Pulitzer Prize-winning natural history and evolutionary biology toolkit chronicling Peter and Rosemary Grant's decades-long research on Darwin's finches, showing evolution happening in real time through drought, food availability, and natural selection.
useCase: Understand evolution through the most detailed study of natural selection ever conducted — how finches on a remote Galapagos island show evolution happening in real time.
knownRisks: The book focuses heavily on the Grants' research — not a comprehensive survey of evolutionary biology
Some evolutionary concepts are simplified for general readers
The 1977 drought was an extreme event — not all selection pressures are that strong
